story.iWant: to drag-and-drop my preset file onto a web page
story.soThat: I can quickly start the conversion process without hunting for a file picker button

tasks.task: Create HTML page structure with header, main drop zone, and footer
Implement CSS styling for drop zone states (default, hover, dragover, success, error)
Add drag-drop event handlers in JavaScript (file-handler.js)
Implement file picker fallback with keyboard accessibility
Implement responsive layout for desktop, tablet, mobile
Add WASM loader boilerplate (loads but doesn't execute conversion)
Manual testing of all acceptance criteria

criterion.title: Page Structure and Branding

requirements.requirement: HTML page loads with Recipe branding ("🍳 Recipe" title)
Hero section with 3-sentence description: Convert photo presets between formats, Nikon NP3 ↔ Lightroom XMP ↔ lrtemplate, 100% privacy - your files never leave your device
Footer with version number and GitHub link

criterion.test: Open http://localhost:8080 → see branded page with clear purpose

criterion.title: Drag-Drop Zone Visual Design
criterion.test: Visual inspection - drop zone is immediately obvious on page load

criterion.title: Drag-Drop Interaction States

requirements.requirement: Default state: Dashed border, neutral background
Hover state (dragover): Solid border, highlighted background (e.g., light blue)
Active state (file dropped): Border changes to success color (green)
Error state: Border changes to error color (red) with error message

criterion.test: 1. Drag file over page → hover state activates 2. Drag file over drop zone → hover state intensifies 3. Drop valid file → success state 4. Drop invalid file (e.g., .jpg) → error state

criterion.title: File Picker Fallback

requirements.requirement: Click anywhere on drop zone → native file picker opens
Selected file triggers same upload flow as drag-drop

criterion.test: 1. Click drop zone → file picker opens 2. Select .xmp file → file uploaded 3. Select .jpg file → error message (handled in Story 2-2)

criterion.title: Keyboard Accessibility

requirements.requirement: Drop zone is focusable via Tab key

criterion.test: Tab to drop zone, press Enter → file picker opens

criterion.title: File Validation (Basic)

requirements.requirement: Display error for other file types: "Please upload a preset file (.np3, .xmp, or .lrtemplate)"
Error message is dismissible (click to close)

criterion.test: 1. Drop image.jpg → error: "Please upload a preset file" 2. Drop preset.xmp → no error (passes to Story 2-2)

criterion.title: Responsive Layout (Desktop/Tablet)

requirements.requirement: Mobile (<768px): Drop zone full width, smaller text

criterion.test: Resize browser window → layout adapts without breaking

doc.title: Technical Specification: Epic 2 - Web Interface
doc.snippet: Story 2-1 delivers basic page structure with drag-drop zone. Deliverables: web/index.html (replace test version), web/static/style.css, Drag-drop visual feedback, File picker fallback. Complexity: Simple (1-2 days).

doc.title: Technical Specification: Epic 2 - Web Interface
doc.snippet: Decision: Vanilla JavaScript (zero dependencies). Epic 2 UI is simple (10 stories, minimal state). Zero framework overhead (target: <100KB total JS). Faster initial load time. Can refactor to framework in Epic 2+ if complexity grows.

doc.title: Technical Specification: Epic 2 - Web Interface
doc.snippet: Decision: Eager loading + Service Worker caching. Users come to site to convert (not to browse). Loading WASM upfront sets expectation. Implementation: Load WASM on page load with Go runtime, status message "Loading converter..."

doc.title: Technical Specification: Epic 2 - Web Interface
doc.snippet: Proposed structure: web/index.html (Story 2-1), web/static/style.css (Story 2-1, 2-10), web/static/main.js, web/static/file-handler.js (Story 2-2), web/static/recipe.wasm (1.03MB compressed), web/static/wasm_exec.js (~16KB). Code organization: ES6 modules (import/export), no bundler for MVP.

doc.title: Technical Specification: Epic 2 - Web Interface
doc.snippet: Support Matrix: Chrome/Firefox/Safari/Edge (Latest 2) - Full support. Required features: WebAssembly (2017+), File API (FileReader, Blob, URL.createObjectURL), Drag-and-drop events, Promises/async-await. IE11: Not supported (no WASM).

doc.title: WASM Preparation Report - Epic 2 Readiness
doc.snippet: Successfully compiled Recipe to WebAssembly. Binary size: Uncompressed 3.7MB, Compressed (gzip) 1.03MB (66% under 3MB target). WASM entry point: cmd/wasm/main.go exposes convert(), detectFormat(), getVersion() functions. Build scripts ready.

doc.title: WASM Preparation Report - Epic 2 Readiness
doc.snippet: Sample files available: 22 NP3, 913 XMP, 544 lrtemplate (1,479 total). Test workflow: Start server (python web/serve.py or node web/serve.js), open http://localhost:8080, upload file, convert, download, observe timing.

doc.title: recipe - Product Requirements Document
doc.snippet: Recipe is a universal photo preset converter solving a critical gap for Nikon Z-series users. Browser-based conversion tool powered by WebAssembly with bidirectional conversion and 95%+ accuracy. Privacy-first: all processing happens in-browser, files never leave user's device.

doc.title: Architecture
doc.snippet: Frontend Language: Vanilla JavaScript (ES6+), No frameworks. Rationale: Zero build step, simple deployment, <100ms WASM goal achievable. Project Structure: cmd/{cli,tui,wasm}/ + internal/ + web/. Frontend uses FileReader API → ArrayBuffer pattern.

artifact.reason: WASM entry point exposing Recipe conversion engine to JavaScript. Story 2-1 needs to load this WASM module on page load and call getVersion() to display in footer.
Existing minimal test interface. Story 2-1 will replace this with production-quality HTML structure including drag-drop zone, proper branding, and accessibility features.
Go WASM runtime glue code (~16KB). Required for loading recipe.wasm. Must be included in HTML before main.js.
WASM build script. Developers need to run this to compile recipe.wasm before testing Story 2-1 implementation. Builds cmd/wasm/main.go → web/static/recipe.wasm
Python development server for local testing. Story 2-1 acceptance criteria require testing at http://localhost:8080. Alternative to serve.js.
Node.js development server for local testing. Alternative to serve.py for developers who prefer Node.js.

go.note: Required for WASM compilation (go build -o recipe.wasm). Story 2-1 doesn't modify Go code but needs WASM module loaded.

javascript.note: Zero npm dependencies - Vanilla JavaScript with browser built-in APIs

browser-apis.api: Drag and Drop API (dragenter, dragover, dragleave, drop events)
URL API (URL.createObjectURL for downloads in future stories)
Custom Events (wasmReady event from WASM module)

build-tools.tool: Python 3.x or Node.js (for dev server: serve.py or serve.js)

constraint.rule: Use Vanilla JavaScript only - NO frameworks (React, Vue, Svelte, etc.)
constraint.rationale: Epic 2 UI is simple (10 stories), zero framework overhead keeps total JS <100KB, faster initial load, easier to understand for contributors

constraint.rule: ES6 modules with import/export. NO bundler for MVP (native browser support)
constraint.rationale: Simple deployment, can add webpack/rollup later if needed. Files: main.js imports from file-handler.js, wasm-loader.js, etc.

constraint.rule: Support Chrome, Firefox, Safari, Edge (latest 2 versions). NO IE11 support.
constraint.rationale: Required features: WebAssembly (2017+), File API, drag-drop events, Promises/async-await. IE11 lacks WASM.

constraint.rule: Desktop-first design (≥1024px primary). Tablet secondary (768-1023px). Mobile minimal (<768px).
constraint.rationale: Target users are photographers working on desktops/laptops. Mobile is not primary use case per PRD.

constraint.rule: Zero network requests during conversion. All processing client-side. No analytics or tracking.
constraint.rationale: Core value proposition: "Your files never leave your device." Must be verifiable via browser DevTools Network tab.

constraint.rule: Keyboard navigation required. Tab to drop zone, Enter/Space opens file picker. ARIA labels for screen readers.
constraint.rationale: AC-5 requires keyboard accessibility. Focus indicators, proper semantic HTML, role attributes.

constraint.rule: Accept only .np3, .xmp, .lrtemplate file extensions. Display user-friendly error for other files.
constraint.rationale: AC-6 requires basic validation in Story 2-1. Story 2-3 will add format detection via WASM.

constraint.rule: Eager loading on page load. Display "Loading converter..." status message. Load before user can interact.
constraint.rationale: Users come to site to convert. Loading WASM upfront sets expectation. Service Worker caching for subsequent visits.

interface.name: convert
interface.signature: convert(inputBytes: Uint8Array, fromFormat: string, toFormat: string): Promise<Uint8Array>
interface.description: Performs format conversion. Story 2-1 does NOT call this function - it's loaded but not used until Story 2-6. However, WASM module must load successfully for getVersion() to work.

interface.name: detectFormat
interface.description: Auto-detects file format (returns "np3", "xmp", or "lrtemplate"). Story 2-1 does NOT call this - it's used in Story 2-3.

interface.name: getVersion
interface.description: Returns Recipe version string (e.g., "1.0.0-wasm"). Story 2-1 MUST call this to display version in footer per AC-1. Non-async, returns immediately.

interface.name: wasmReady event
interface.description: Fired when WASM module loads successfully. Story 2-1 should listen for this event to update status banner from "Loading..." to "Ready" and enable drop zone.

interface.name: File API
interface.description: Story 2-1 uses File API for file picker and drag-drop but does NOT read file contents yet. Story 2-2 will implement FileReader to convert File → Uint8Array.

tests.standards:
Epic 2 uses manual testing with browser DevTools. Automated E2E tests are deferred to Epic 6 (Validation & Testing). Story 2-1 testing approach:
**Browser Compatibility Testing:** - Test in Chrome, Firefox, Safari (latest 2 versions each) - Verify identical behavior across all browsers - Use browser DevTools for debugging
**Accessibility Testing:** - Screen reader testing (NVDA/JAWS) to verify ARIA labels - Keyboard-only navigation (Tab, Enter, Space keys) - Verify focus indicators are visible - Check role attributes (role="button", role="alert")
**Responsive Design Testing:** - Test at 3 breakpoints: Desktop (1920px), Tablet (800px), Mobile (500px) - Verify no horizontal scrollbars at any viewport size - Check that layout adapts gracefully
**Code Review Standards (from Epic 1 Retrospective):** - Self-review sufficient for simple stories like 2-1 - Check for console errors in browser DevTools - Verify all acceptance criteria met before marking done - Test with real sample files from examples/ directory
**Local Development:** - Start server: python web/serve.py or node web/serve.js - Open http://localhost:8080 - Use examples/ directory files for testing - Check Network tab for zero requests during interaction

locations.note: No automated test files for Epic 2 - manual testing only. Future: Epic 6 will add Playwright E2E tests.

test.idea: Open http://localhost:8080 and verify: Recipe branding visible, hero section with 3-sentence description present, footer shows version number (from getVersion()) and GitHub link
Visual inspection on page load: Drop zone is 400×300px minimum, centered, has dashed border, shows 📁 icon and "Drop your preset file here" text, displays supported formats (.np3, .xmp, .lrtemplate)

1) Drag file over page (not drop zone) → no hover effect. 2) Drag file over drop zone → solid border, highlighted background. 3) Drop valid .xmp file → green border (success state). 4) Drop invalid .jpg file → red border (error state) with error message

Click drop zone → native file picker opens. Select .lrtemplate file → success state. Verify file picker has accept=".np3,.xmp,.lrtemplate" attribute. Test that file picker selection triggers same flow as drag-drop
Press Tab until drop zone receives focus → verify clear outline. Press Enter → file picker opens. Press Space → file picker opens. Screen reader announces "Upload preset file" when focused

1) Drop image.jpg → error: "Please upload a preset file (.np3, .xmp, or .lrtemplate)". 2) Drop preset.xmp → no error. 3) Error message is dismissible. 4) Error disappears after 3 seconds automatically
Resize browser window: 1920px → drop zone 600px wide centered. 800px → drop zone 90% width. 500px → drop zone full width with smaller text. No horizontal scrollbars at any size
Test all 7 ACs in Chrome, Firefox, and Safari. Verify WASM loads successfully (check "Recipe WASM module loaded successfully" in console). Verify no console errors on page load
On page load, status banner shows "Loading converter...". When WASM loads, status changes to "Ready". Footer displays version from getVersion(). Listen for wasmReady event
